data.title: South Korean antitrust regulators visit Arm’s office in Seoul: report
data.description:
Officials from South Korea’s antitrust regulator visited the Seoul offices of Arm (ARM) this week as part of ongoing scrutiny of the British chip designer's licensing practices, Bloomberg News reported, citing people  with knowledge of the matter.

The Korea Fair Trade Commission is carrying out the unannounced inspections of Arm as part of an inquiry into its conduct, the report added.

The move comes in part from a complaint by Qualcomm (QCOM)  alleging that Arm is hurting competition by restricting access to its technology after operating an open network for over 20 years, the report noted.

Arm and Qualcomm did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The American chipmaker has also brought its concerns about Arm’s licensing business to the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and European Commission, according to the report.

Qualcomm alleges that Arm formed a reliance on its technology via an open licensing model, which also fostered a thriving chip industry, the report noted.

Arm, which is majority-owned by SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF), sells chip designs and licenses a so-called instruction set, a code used by software to communicate with processors. Chipmakers like Qualcomm and device makers such as Apple (AAPL) rely on Arm licenses for their products.

Qualcomm is telling competition authorities globally that the dynamic market is now threatened as Arm curbs access to boost profits through its own chipmaking ambitions, Bloomberg previously reported. Arm previously said Qualcomm’s complaints to regulators look to “expand the parties’ ongoing commercial dispute for its own competitive benefit,” the report noted.

Both companies are trying to position themselves to ride a wave in computing demand, while the market for smartphone chips, which had fueled much of their growth in recent years, has become slow.

The two companies are in a legal tussle globally. Qualcomm largely prevailed in a trial late last year over claims by Arm that it breached its licensing agreement. Meanwhile, last month, Arm appealed a judge's ruling in a licensing dispute against Qualcomm that left the American company's jury victory intact.

Qualcomm’s complaints to regulators are confidential, but in court filings last year, the company noted that after Arm’s acquisition by Japan’s SoftBank and the failure of an attempted sale to Nvidia (NVDA), Arm is acting anticompetitively, the report added.

data.title: BlackRock declares $5.21 dividend
data.description:
• BlackRock (BLK) declares $5.21/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable Dec. 23; for shareholders of record Dec. 5; ex-div Dec. 5.
  • See BLK:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Global-E Online beats top-line estimates; gives Q4 and updates FY outlook
data.description:
• Global-E Online press release (GLBE): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.07 in-line.
  • Revenue of $220.78M (+25.5% Y/Y) beats by $2.92M.
  • GMV1 in the third quarter of 2025 was $1,512 million, an increase of 33% year over year.
  • Non-GAAP Gross Margin2 in the third quarter of 2025 was 46.3%, compared with 46.7% in the third quarter of 2024. GAAP Gross Margin in the third quarter of 2025 was 45.1%
  • Adjusted EBITDA3 in the third quarter of 2025 was $41.3 million compared to $31.1 million in the third quarter of 2024, an increase of 33% year over year.
  • Net cash from operating activities in the third quarter of 2025 was $74.3 million compared with $30.3 million in the third quarter of 2024
  • Free Cash Flow in the third quarter of 2025 was $73.6 million, compared with $29.9 million in the third quarter of 2024.

  • Q4 2025 and Full Year Outlook

    Global-e is introducing fourth quarter guidance and is raising the full year guidance as follows:

      Q4 2025   FY 2025   Previous FY 2025
    (in millions)
    GMV(1) $2,195 - $2,315   $6,404 - $6,524   $6,220 - $6,520
    Revenue $318.5 - $334.5 (consensus of $321.79M)   $944.1 - $960.1 (consensus of $945.61M)   $921.5 - $971.5
    Adjusted EBITDA(3) $74.3 - $88.7   $185.6 - $200.0   $180 - $200

data.title: Lowe's reports mixed Q3 results; raises FY25 sales outlook
data.description:
• Lowe's press release (LOW): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.06 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $20.81B (+3.2% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Comparable sales for the quarter increased 0.4%, driven by 11.4% online sales growth, double-digit growth in home services and continued growth in Pro sales.
  • As of Oct. 31, 2025, Lowe's operated 1,756 stores, representing 195.8 million square feet of retail selling space.

Lowe's Business Outlook

The company is updating its outlook for the operating results of full year 2025 to reflect the ongoing uncertainty in the macroeconomic environment. The updated outlook also includes expectations for FBM. 

Adjusted operating income, adjusted operating margin and adjusted diluted EPS are non-GAAP financial measures that exclude the transaction costs, purchase accounting adjustments and intangible asset amortization related to the acquisition of FBM and ADG. The company does not provide a reconciliation for non-GAAP estimates on a forward-looking basis where it is unable to provide a meaningful or accurate calculation or estimation of reconciling items (which may be significant) without unreasonable effort.

Full Year 2025 Outlook

  • Total sales of $86.0 billion (previously $84.5 to $85.5 billion) vs $85.66B consensus.
  • Comparable sales expected to be flat as compared to prior year (previously flat to up +1%)
  • Adjusted operating income as a percentage of sales (adjusted operating margin) of 12.1%(previously 12.2% to 12.3%)
  • Net interest expense of approximately $1.4 billion (previously $1.3 billion)
  • Effective income tax rate of approximately 24.0% (previously 24.5%)
  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share of approximately $12.25 (previously $12.20 to $12.45) vs $12.27 consensus
  • Capital expenditures of up to $2.5 billion

data.title: Boeing inks $13 billion deal with Flydubai for 737 Max Jets
data.description:
• Boeing (BA) has reached a deal with Flydubai for up to 150 jets, following the airline’s first-ever order with Airbus SE earlier this week.
  • The memorandum of understanding on Wednesday, which Flydubai valued at $13 billion, includes 75 of the U.S. planemaker’s 737 Max jets and options for 75 more.
  • Flydubai currently operates 96 Boeing 737 airplanes. The new agreement, when finalized, would add to the outstanding 737 MAX orders from prior purchases.

data.title: Here's what to watch as Nvidia reports earnings today
data.description:
AI bubble talk has been all the rage recently, along with the crypto crash and equity losing streaks, but the best pulse check will likely come today as artificial intelligence stalwart Nvidia (NVDA) reports earnings. The company's figures are watched far and wide, not only because they can impact hundreds of ETFs, but because of growing discussion surrounding rich valuations. It won't be the only thing on investors' minds this week amid the delayed release of September's jobs report and decreased expectations for Fed rate cuts, but the numbers will surely be important to the overall trajectory of the market (Nvidia accounts for about 7% of the S&P 500). 

Commentary: Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid highlights that Nvidia has now fallen more than 12% from its October 29 peak, marking the sharpest drop since the “Liberation Day” volatility earlier this year. He also notes that even major new AI deals failed to lift sentiment: Nvidia committed up to $10B to Anthropic and Microsoft up to $5B, with Anthropic set to buy $30B in Azure compute, yet both stocks declined on the day. Additionally, Reid outlined, "sentiment has turned more negative in the last few weeks, with the circular AI deals being treated with increasing caution as the conversation around a potential bubble has gathered pace." Feel-good era of AI deals may be gone already

Elsewhere, there are worries over the Trump administration’s push to curb Nvidia exports to China, as well as bouts of institutional selling. Softbank (OTCPK:SFTBY) and Peter Thiel's hedge fund recently dumped their Nvidia (NVDA) shares, while Michael Burry took a prominent bet against the company (before shuttering his fund). While those concerns may be justified, it's hard to pinpoint exact reasons that may include reallocations of capital or rotating positions that can still focus on the AI trade.

What do investors need to hear? A beat and raise is expected, but the bigger focus will be on whether AI spending is slowing. CEO Jensen Huang will need to provide clear details on Nvidia's (NVDA) order book and visibility surrounding long-term backlog. It was only three weeks ago that he revealed a "half a trillion dollar" cumulative figure at GTC, which propelled Nvidia (NVDA) to a world-first $5T valuation. The free cash flow story will also be key, as well as strong margins that indicate pricing power and insatiable capacity demand from hyperscalers.

Volatility is juicing premiums: According to Cboe Global Markets data, options traders are positioning for a big Nvidia (NVDA) stock swing, anticipating an 8% move in either direction, following earnings and through Friday.

data.title: Thanksgiving dinner cost declines for third straight year: AFBF survey
data.description:
The cost of a Thanksgiving dinner will be lower this year, marking the third consecutive annual decline in holiday meal costs, according to the American Farm Bureau Federation.

The AFBF estimated the cost of a holiday meal for 10 this year would be $55.18 (or about $5.52 per person), down 5% from 2024.

The price of a Thanksgiving turkey saw a steep drop this year, helping pull overall meal costs lower. A 16-pound frozen bird now averages $21.50, or $1.34 per pound - a decline of more than 16% from a year ago.

The estimates are based on a survey by AFBF volunteer shoppers, who checked the prices of items used in a typical Thanksgiving dinner in the first week of November.

The bureau’s survey menu has remained unchanged since it began conducting the yearly informal survey in 1986 to allow for consistent price comparisons. It has 12 items, including stuffing mix, pie crusts, dinner rolls, sweet potatoes, and cranberries.

“While the wholesale price for fresh turkey is up from 2024, grocery stores are featuring Thanksgiving deals and attempting to draw consumer demand back to turkey, leading to lower retail prices for a holiday bird,” the AFBF said in a statement.

The bureau noted that the average per-pound feature price for whole frozen turkeys dropped further in the second week of November, citing USDA Agricultural Marketing Service data.

However, despite three consecutive years of declining costs, the price of a Thanksgiving dinner still hasn’t fully eased from a sharp jump in 2022 to a record $64.05, according to the AFBF.

Food prices remain a point of concern, especially heading into Thanksgiving and a record-long government shutdown delaying economic data. The most recent CPI report, from September, showed inflation running at 3%, the highest since January. Many household staples saw their biggest monthly increases in three years, with more than half the items in the index rising more than 3%.

Major publicly listed retailers central to U.S. Thanksgiving dinner shopping include Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Kroger (KR), Albertsons (ACI), Costco (COST), BJ’s Wholesale (BJ), Dollar General (DG), Dollar Tree (DLTR), and Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM). Key turkey and holiday-food suppliers include Hormel (HRL), Tyson (TSN), and Pilgrim’s Pride (PPC).

data.title: Michael Burry Says He’s Still Trading — Bloomberg
data.description:
Michael Burry says he remains active in markets even as Scion Asset Management relinquishes its registration with the Securities and Exchange Commission, Bloomberg reported.

In an email to Bloomberg News, Burry said the most recent iteration of Scion operated as “essentially a friends and family fund” and was never intended to gather outside capital.

“I didn’t market it or treat it like most do,” he said. “I am still running my money and active in markets.”

Burry formally ended Scion’s RIA status earlier this month and hinted on X that he was “on to much better things Nov. 25.” He emphasized that Scion Asset Management is not shutting down, but will no longer manage money for external investors.

The 54-year-old investor — best known for his winning bet against subprime mortgages before the 2008 financial crisis — continues to draw attention for his market commentary and regulatory filings. Recent disclosures showed bearish positions in Nvidia (NVDA) and Palantir (PLTR), along with call options tied to Halliburton (HAL) and Pfizer (PFE).

Burry added he welcomed being “free of the compliance burden and broad misunderstandings generated by my filings.”

data.title: British Land Company reports 1H results
data.description:
• British Land Company press release (BTLCY): 1H GAAP EPS of 15.4p, up 1% (HY25: 15.3p)
  • Underlying Profit £155m, up 8% (HY25: £143m)
  • £59m of assets disposed at an average of 5% above book value
  • £52m of retail acquired, principally two retail parks, at 8.4% Topped Up NIY
  • OUTLOOK: Like-for-like net rental growth expected to be c.5% for FY26; Reiterating guidance of 3-5% per annum ERV growth across the portfolio; Expect FY26 Underlying EPS of at least 28.5p, with growth of at least 6% expected for FY27 (30.2p), and 3-6% beyond; Comfortable with current market expectations for Underlying EP

data.title: More U.S. homes are losing value, but it's not a crash - Zillow
data.description:
Home values have declined for more than half of U.S. households in the past year, according to Zillow (Z), marking the highest share since the end of the 2000s housing crisis.

Zillow (Z) estimates that 53% of homes lost value over the past year as of October, up from 14% a year ago. That's the highest share of homes declining in value since April 2012, when the housing crash was starting to bottom out.

"Home values surged over the past six years, and the vast majority of homeowners still have significant equity," said Treh Manhertz, senior economic researcher, Zillow (Z). "What we're seeing now is a normalization, not a crash."

The vast majority of U.S. homeowners have seen their home values rise substantially in the time they've owned them - 67% growth at the median.

Manhertz said relatively few homeowners are selling at a loss - 4.1% of homes have lost value since their last sale, a smaller share than before the pandemic. 

Notably, loss of value over the past year has been most widespread in the West and South, while the losses have largely missed the Northeast and Midwest so far.
